_help._note: Allowed values + requirements per field (this _help block is ignored by the gate — keep or delete). Run `plumb schema` for the full reference.
_help.task_id: required — ticket/issue/branch id (also the receipt filename)
_help.agent_id: required — which agent or human did the work
_help.intent: required — what + why, plain language — the semantic review reads this
_help.self_modifying: one of: true | false — required — MUST be true if changed_files touch policy.protected_paths; touching one with false is a hard fail; true escalates to human review
_help.validation_plan[].required: one of: true | false — required — is this check mandatory
_help.execution_evidence: required — each: { command, status, output_ref?, skip_reason? }
_help.execution_evidence[].status: one of: passed | failed | skipped — required — required steps must be 'passed'; use skip_reason when 'skipped'
_help.changed_files: required — set by `plumb receipt --write` — don't hand-edit
_help.diff_sha256: required — set by `plumb receipt --write` — never hand-edit
_help.result_summary: required — what changed + how it was verified

intent: Follow-up to the merged CI + Plumbline gate (PR #1). The Plumbline Gate finishes in ~15s while the Rust CI (Lint & Unit Tests) takes minutes, so on every first PR run the gate's ci-evidence check raced ahead and reported FAIL (required check 'Lint & Unit Tests' status=in_progress), forcing a manual re-run. This adds a step to plumbline.yml that polls the required 'Lint & Unit Tests' check-run to a terminal state (up to 20 min) before invoking the gate, so it evaluates real CI evidence exactly once and no longer flaps. Pure workflow ergonomics; no change to the gate's judgment logic.

validation_plan.reason: the change is YAML-only; confirm the workflow still parses

validation_plan.command: Plumbline Gate on this PR reaches ci-evidence PASS without a manual re-run
validation_plan.reason: the whole point of the wait step is that the gate no longer races the Rust CI on first run

execution_evidence.command: Plumbline Gate on this PR reaches ci-evidence PASS without a manual re-run
execution_evidence.output_ref: gate waited for 'Lint & Unit Tests' to finish, then shape+ci-evidence PASS; semantic review escalate (self_modifying) — see PR gate comment

result_summary: Added a 'Wait for required CI check' step to .github/workflows/plumbline.yml that polls the 'Lint & Unit Tests' check-run to a terminal state before the gate runs, eliminating the ci-evidence race seen on PR #1 (gate finished in ~15s and reported FAIL while the Rust build was still in_progress). YAML verified to parse; behavior verified on this PR's own gate run. Touches .github/workflows/** (protected) so self_modifying:true; the gate escalates to human review by design. Do not auto-merge.
